front: Novel: narrator learns his real father is not 'the Scholarly Attorney'; features Anne Stanton and Judge Irwin
back: All the King's Men (Robert Penn Warren)

front: Poem: folk-ballad in which a birthmark 'shaped for luck' allows a serial killer's wife to identify their returning son
back: "The Ballad of Billie Potts" (Robert Penn Warren)

back: "Evening Hawk" and related poems (Robert Penn Warren)

front: Author: co-founded the Southern Review with Cleanth Brooks; co-authored the textbook Understanding Fiction
Author: contributed the pro-segregationist essay 'The Briar Patch' to I'll Take My Stand but later became an integrationist
Short Story: a tramp works on Seth's parents' farm in Tennessee during a cold spell in June; protagonist visits Old Jebb and bedridden Dellie
Novel: based on the Kentucky Tragedy; features the character Jeremiah Beaumont
back: World Enough and Time (Robert Penn Warren)
